file_insights.file_purpose: Core Prisma schema defining multi-tenant system entities
key_elements: User model with optional organization membership
Role model with platform and organization scopes
Session model for JWT refresh token management
architectural_patterns: Multi-tenant architecture with organizationId as tenant discriminator
JWT session management with refresh tokens
Role-based access control (RBAC) with granular permissions
Soft delete pattern with deletedAt field
Compound unique constraints for scoped entities

patterns_discovered.description: organizationId used as discriminator for all tenant-scoped entities. Platform-level users have null organizationId.
patterns_discovered.implementation: Organization model as root, all related models reference organizationId with optional relationships for platform entities

patterns_discovered.description: All core entities include deletedAt DateTime field for logical deletion instead of hard deletion
patterns_discovered.implementation: deletedAt field with indexes on status/deletedAt combinations for efficient querying

patterns_discovered.description: Two-tier permission system: Roles with scope (PLATFORM/ORGANIZATION) mapped to granular Permissions via junction table
patterns_discovered.implementation: Role -> RolePermission -> Permission hierarchy with scope-based isolation

patterns_discovered.description: Entities with scope field (Role, Permission) can exist at platform or organization level
patterns_discovered.implementation: scope enum field with compound unique constraints on name+scope+organizationId

patterns_discovered.description: System-wide audit trail capturing all significant actions with metadata
patterns_discovered.implementation: AuditLog model with action enum, resource tracking, old/new values, and request context (IP, userAgent)

patterns_discovered.description: Device-aware session tracking with refresh token-based authentication
patterns_discovered.implementation: Session model with userAgent, ipAddress, expiresAt, and lastUsedAt for security and analytics

patterns_discovered.description: Many-to-many relationships via explicit junction models (UserRole, RolePermission)
patterns_discovered.implementation: Composite unique constraints and cascading deletes with appropriate indexing

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Duplicate relation names in User model
gotchas_discovered.description: User model has 'assignedRoles' relation that reuses 'UserRole' with different name from 'userRoles'. The assignedRoles appears to be for tracking role assignments made by a user, not roles assigned to the user.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Potential confusion about which UserRole relation represents what. The 'assignedRoles' relation uses @relation('RoleAssigner') which maps to assignedBy field, so it's tracking assignments made BY the user, not TO the user.
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Consider renaming to clarify: userRoles (roles assigned to user) vs assignedRoles (assignments made by user) or provide documentation

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Missing cascade delete for Organization references
gotchas_discovered.description: Some models reference Organization without onDelete: Cascade constraint. If organization is deleted, related records might orphan or cause referential integrity issues.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Cascading deletes are not specified for Organization relations in User, Role, Menu, Category, etc., potentially leaving orphaned records
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Add onDelete: Cascade to all Organization foreign keys or explicitly define deletion behavior

gotchas_discovered.description: Organization model references 'Plan' entity via planId field, but Plan model is not defined in this schema file
gotchas_discovered.impact: Compile-time or runtime errors if Plan model doesn't exist in other schema files or if models are not properly composed
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Ensure Plan model is defined in another schema file and schema files are properly concatenated/composed in prisma.schema

gotchas_discovered.description: Organization model references multiple undefined models: Subscription, Menu, Category, Product, Table, Zone, QRCode, Order, Customer, Theme, Media, Notification
gotchas_discovered.impact: Schema compilation will fail if referenced models are not defined in composed schema files
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Verify all referenced models exist in other schema files and that schema composition is properly configured

gotchas_discovered.description: AuditLog model uses 'AuditAction' enum type which is not defined in this schema file
gotchas_discovered.impact: Schema will not compile without enum definition
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Define AuditAction enum (CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, READ, etc.) in schema or separate enums file

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: No cascade delete for User in Session
gotchas_discovered.description: Session has onDelete: Cascade but related models might have different deletion strategies
gotchas_discovered.impact: Inconsistent cascade delete behavior across related entities

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created comprehensive core.prisma schema with all required entities (Organization, User, Role, Permission, Session) and supporting junction tables. Implementation follows multi-tenant architecture patterns with proper soft delete, RBAC, and audit logging capabilities.
deliverables_completed: Organization model as tenant root with relationships to all tenant-scoped data
User model with support for platform and organization users
Role model with scope-based differentiation (PLATFORM vs ORGANIZATION)
Session model with refresh token and device tracking
UserRole junction table with assignment tracking
Comprehensive indexing strategy for query optimization
Documentation comments explaining each entity's purpose

recommendations.recommendation: Define missing enum types (AuditAction, UserStatus, OrganizationStatus, RoleScope) at the beginning of core.prisma or in a separate enums file to ensure schema compilation
action_items: Define AuditAction enum with values: CREATE, READ, UPDATE, DELETE, LOGIN, LOGOUT, etc.
Define UserStatus enum: ACTIVE, INACTIVE, SUSPENDED
Define OrganizationStatus enum: ACTIVE, TRIAL, SUSPENDED, CANCELED

recommendations.recommendation: Add onDelete: Cascade to all foreign key relationships referencing Organization to ensure data consistency on tenant deletion
action_items: Add cascade deletes to all menu, category, product, order references

recommendations.recommendation: Verify prisma/schema.prisma properly includes all schema files (core.prisma + referenced entity files) and document the composition strategy
action_items: Check prisma.schema for proper schema includes
Document which models are in which files
Ensure no circular dependencies between schema files

recommendations.recommendation: Add documentation for the implicit relationships between entities, especially the distinction between userRoles and assignedRoles in User model
action_items: Add comment explaining userRoles vs assignedRoles purpose
Document soft delete query patterns (use 'where: { deletedAt: null }')

recommendations.recommendation: Review indexing strategy for common query patterns on compound keys and timestamp filters
action_items: Consider adding combined index on (organizationId, status) for fast user lookups
Verify timestamp indexes support range queries for audit logs
Consider partial indexes on deletedAt for soft delete queries

recommendations.recommendation: Add database-level constraints for refreshToken uniqueness and session validity enforcement
action_items: Ensure refreshToken field is indexed for fast lookups
Consider adding constraint on expiresAt > now() for valid sessions
Document password hashing requirements for passwordHash field
